title: 10 best herbal capsules for low sperm count
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for low sperm count are natural supplements that contain plant-based ingredients designed to boost male fertility. These capsules work by enhancing sperm quality, increasing sperm motility, and improving overall reproductive health. For example, Tribulus terrestris and Ashwagandha have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to improve sperm count and semen quality. By taking these herbal capsules, men with low sperm count can expect improved fertility and conception rates. This means reduced stress and anxiety for individuals struggling with infertility, allowing them to focus on building a family and enjoying their lives to the fullest.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Puncture vine capsules helps with low sperm count because they are rich in Tribulus terrestris, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to boost male fertility. The capsules contain saponins, which stimulate the pituitary gland and hypothalamus to increase testosterone production, leading to improved spermatogenesis and enhanced sperm motility. Additionally, puncture vine capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress in the testes, creating a favorable environment for healthy sperm development and count. This natural supplement has shown promise in increasing sperm count and improving fertility in men with low sperm counts.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Tribulus terrestris has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which helps protect the testes and reproductive organs from damage caused by free radicals, potentially improving sperm quality and count.

Hormone regulation: Puncture vine is believed to help regulate hormone levels in the body, including testosterone, which plays a crucial role in sperm production and development, thereby supporting healthy sperm count.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The capsules have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the reproductive tract, improving fertility and potentially increasing sperm count.
remedy_constituents: Furostanol saponins: Furostanol saponins in Tribulus terrestris capsules have been found to increase testosterone levels, which is essential for sperm production and motility.
Protodioscin: Protodioscin, a type of furostanol saponin, has been shown to improve sperm count and motility by stimulating the production of sex hormones and enhancing sperm function.

Terrestrisone: Terrestrisone, another saponin present in Tribulus terrestris, has been reported to have a positive effect on sperm parameters, including increased sperm count and motility, likely due to its ability to enhance testosterone production and sperm function.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and saponins, which are believed to support reproductive health.
Roots: They are used for their purported adaptogenic and antioxidant properties, which may help improve overall fertility and sperm quality.
Flowers: They are used for their saponin content, which may help increase testosterone levels and improve sperm count and motility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 pounds of dried tribulus terrestris roots and stems in late summer or early fall season.
Clean the dried roots and stems by rinsing them with distilled water and pat dry with paper towels.
Grind the cleaned tribulus terrestris roots and stems into a fine powder using a grinder or coffee mill for 2-3 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the powdered tribulus terrestris and seal each capsule immediately.
Label and store the herbal tribulus terrestris capsules in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with low sperm count because it is a natural stress-reliever that reduces anxiety and cortisol levels, which are common contributors to low sperm count. By promoting relaxation and reducing oxidative stress, ashwagandha capsules may improve sperm quality and increase motility. Additionally, ashwagandha's ability to regulate blood sugar levels and improve testosterone production can also help boost fertility and enhance overall reproductive health, ultimately increasing the chances of conception for individuals with low sperm count.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Ashwagandha helps to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to low sperm count by increasing cortisol levels and disrupting hormone balance.

Antioxidant Activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help protect the testes from oxidative stress, damage caused by free radicals, and inflammation, which can lead to low sperm count and poor fertility.

Stress-Reducing Neurotransmitters: Ashwagandha increases the production of neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin, which helps regulate stress response, promoting a healthy nervous system and supporting reproductive health.
remedy_constituents: Withanolides: These alkaloid compounds help increase sperm count by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can damage sperm cells and lead to low fertility.
Withanone: This withanolide has been shown to improve sperm count and motility by regulating the expression of genes involved in spermatogenesis, the process by which sperm cells develop.

Withaferin A: This withanolide has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect sperm cells from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, leading to improved sperm count and motility.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Withania somnifera are rich in bioactive compounds that can help improve fertility and increase sperm count.
Leaves: The leaves of Withania somnifera contain antioxidants and other beneficial compounds that may help improve sperm quality and count.
Seeds: The seeds of Withania somnifera are believed to have adaptogenic properties that can help improve fertility and increase sperm count in men.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried withania somnifera roots from a reputable supplier or cultivate your own.
Dry the harvested roots at 50c for 3 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powder with 5g of gelatin and 10ml of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the herbal powder mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca capsules helps with low sperm count because they contain a unique blend of botanicals that stimulate testosterone production, improve semen quality, and enhance fertility. The capsules' natural ingredients help to increase sperm motility, density, and concentration, leading to a significant improvement in overall reproductive health. Additionally, maca's adaptogenic properties help to balance the body's hormonal levels, which can also contribute to a boost in sperm count and overall male fertility.
remedy_properties: Fertility-stimulating compounds: Maca contains various compounds like polysaccharides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that stimulate the production of seminal fluids, improve sperm quality, and increase fertility in men with low sperm count.

Antioxidant properties: Maca's antioxidant properties help protect sperm from damage caused by free radicals, which can reduce sperm motility and viability, making it an effective supplement for improving overall sperm health.

Adaptogenic effects on stress relief: Maca's adaptogenic properties help alleviate stress-related hormonal imbalances that can contribute to low sperm count and poor semen quality, promoting a healthier balance of hormones in the body.
remedy_constituents: Isothiocyanates: Isothiocyanates present in Maca have been found to improve sperm quality by increasing sperm count and motility, possibly due to their antioxidant properties.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Maca have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the reproductive system and improve sperm count and fertility.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Maca may help improve sperm quality by enhancing sperm motility, count, and morphology, possibly due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that are believed to support fertility and sperm health.
Rhyzomes: They are used as they contain the highest concentration of macamides and macaenes, which are compounds that may help improve fertility.
Roots: They are used as they are the primary source of maca's bioactive compounds and are believed to support hormonal balance, which is essential for fertility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lepidium meyenii roots in the early morning when dew is still present on the plant.
Clean the roots thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or debris for 15 minutes.
Dry the roots in a single layer on paper towels at room temperature for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 mg of the powder and seal them immediately to ensure freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with low sperm count because they contain compounds that promote overall male fertility. The herb's saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides help to improve blood flow to the reproductive organs, increasing testosterone production and enhancing sperm quality. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to reduce stress and anxiety, which can negatively impact fertility. By supporting healthy testosterone levels and reducing oxidative stress, herbal ginseng capsules may help improve low sperm count and increase chances of conception.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress in the testes, which can contribute to low sperm count by protecting sperm from damage and promoting healthy sperm production.

Immunomodulatory Effects: Ginseng's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and improving the environment for sperm development and maturation, leading to increased sperm count and motility.

Adaptogenic Properties: Ginseng's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of low sperm count, by regulating the body's response to stress and promoting overall hormonal balance.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins have been shown to improve sperm quality and count by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive system.
Glycosides: Specifically, ginsenoside Rb1 has been found to have antioxidant and anti-apoptotic properties, which may help protect sperm cells from damage and promote healthy sperm production.

Polysaccharides: The polysaccharide components of Panax ginseng, such as ginsan, have been found to stimulate the immune system and increase the production of sperm cells, potentially leading to improved fertility.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain the highest concentration of ginsenosides, which are believed to improve fertility.
Rhyzomes: They also contain ginsenosides, which may help in increasing sperm count and motility.
Leaves: Although less studied than roots and rhyzomes, leaves are also used in some ginseng supplements to support reproductive health.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100 grams of panax ginseng root for 2 hours at 50 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 5 grams of the powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of water.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes at 80 degrees celsius to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto capsules helps with low sperm count because it has been shown to increase testosterone levels, which in turn can improve sperm production and quality. The herb's ability to block the conversion of testosterone into dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to infertility, also plays a role. Additionally, saw palmetto may help reduce inflammation in the reproductive tract, creating a more conducive environment for healthy sperm development and function. As a result, saw palmetto capsules have been traditionally used to support male fertility and alleviate symptoms of low sperm count.
remedy_properties: Inhibits 5-alpha-reductase enzyme: Saw palmetto's fatty acid content inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to low sperm count by shrinking the testes and reducing sperm production.

Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto contains antioxidants that help protect the sperm from oxidative stress and damage, which can occur due to environmental toxins or other factors, leading to low sperm count and poor fertility.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland and reproductive tract, which can contribute to low sperm count and infertility.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Serenoa repens help with low sperm count by improving sperm motility and count due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

Furanosterols: Furanosterols, specifically beta-sitosterol, present in Serenoa repens have been found to improve sperm count and motility by reducing oxidative stress and improving sperm membrane integrity.

Phytosterols: Phytosterols, including beta-sitosterol and campesterol, in Serenoa repens capsules may help with low sperm count by inhibiting the production of testosterone-reducing compounds, thereby promoting a healthy balance of testosterone levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, particularly saponins and phytosterols, which may improve sperm quality and count.
Leaves: High in antioxidants and flavonoids that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healthy sperm production.
Barks: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and glycosides, which may have a positive effect on sperm count and motility.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500 mg of dried serenoa repens into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered serenoa repens with 500 mg of microcrystalline cellulose and 50 mg of silicon dioxide in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and load 250 mg into each capsule shell.
Seal the filled capsule shells using a capsule sealing machine at a pressure of 100 psi.
Store the herbal serenoa repens capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper capsules helps with low sperm count because they contain a natural extract that enhances testosterone levels, promoting healthy sperm production. The active compounds in monk's pepper have been shown to stimulate the pituitary gland, which regulates hormone production, leading to increased fertility. Additionally, monk's pepper has antioxidant properties that help protect the testes from damage caused by free radicals, further supporting healthy sperm development and overall reproductive health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Monk's Pepper capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the sperm from oxidative stress, which can damage DNA and reduce fertility. This property helps to improve sperm quality.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the reproductive tract, which is often associated with low sperm count and poor semen quality. By reducing inflammation, these properties may improve sperm motility and viability.

Antimicrobial Properties: Monk's Pepper contains antimicrobial agents that can help eliminate bacteria and other pathogens from the reproductive tract, which can impair fertility by disrupting sperm function or reducing sperm production. By eliminating these pathogens, these properties may help improve sperm count and overall fertility.
remedy_constituents: Palmarumoside: This iridoid glycoside has been shown to improve sperm quality and motility by increasing the production of sperm and improving their ability to fertilize an egg.

Cnidium monnieri flavonoids: These flavonoids, specifically isorhapontigenin and isorhapontin, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the reproductive system from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to low sperm count.

Furano-diterpenes (Cnidilide): These furano-diterpenes have been found to have anti-apoptotic properties, which help prevent sperm cell death and promote the survival of sperm cells, leading to an improvement in sperm count and quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their adaptogenic properties, which can help improve fertility.
Rhyzomes: Utilized for their bioactive compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, that may enhance sperm quality.
Seeds: Employed for their potential to improve sperm count and motility due to their rich antioxidant and bioactive content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 cnidium monnieri roots of about 0.5-1 cm in diameter and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or mill for 1-2 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered cnidium monnieri with 3 parts of a suitable excipient such as microcrystalline cellulose or starch in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill the mixture into 0.5-1 ml gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine for 10 minutes.
Store the herbal cnidium monnieri capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for 6 months to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with low sperm count because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to improve sperm quality and increase fertility in men. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the testes, which can damage sperm and contribute to low sperm counts. Additionally, licorice root has been found to enhance testosterone levels, promoting healthy sperm production and motility. As a result, herbal licorice capsules may be a natural way to support male fertility and improve chances of conception.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation in the testes and epididymis, improving sperm quality and count.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice root has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals, protecting the sperm cells from damage caused by oxidative stress, thereby promoting healthy sperm production and motility.

Hormone balancing: Herbal licorice capsules contain compounds that have been shown to balance hormone levels, particularly testosterone, which is essential for sperm production and quality, helping to increase sperm count and improve fertility.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules have been shown to improve sperm quality and motility by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the reproductive system, thus potentially increasing fertility.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, present in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect sperm cells from damage and improve sperm count and motility.

Licorice acid (glycyrrhizin): Licorice acid has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the reproductive system, thereby improving sperm quality and count.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which is believed to improve sperm quality and quantity.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help improve sperm count and motility.
Seeds: They are used for their reported antioxidant properties, which may help protect sperm from oxidative stress and improve fertility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra with a shovel in the fall season.
Dry the collected roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 20 grams of gelatin in a bowl and add 10 milliliters of water.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with low sperm count because of its ability to increase testosterone levels in the body. The herb is rich in saponins, which stimulate the production of hormones that enhance sperm motility and quality. Additionally, fenugreek has been shown to improve seminal fluid viscosity, reducing the risk of DNA fragmentation and increasing the chances of successful fertilization. By addressing underlying hormonal imbalances, fenugreek capsules can help boost sperm count and overall fertility in men suffering from low sperm counts.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek helps combat oxidative stress, which can damage sperm DNA and reduce fertility by neutralizing free radicals and protecting sperm from damage.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fenugreek reduces inflammation in the body, which can improve blood flow to the testes and epididymis, enhancing sperm production and motility.
Immunomodulatory effects: Fenugreek stimulates the immune system to produce antibodies that help eliminate abnormal or damaged sperm, leading to an overall improvement in sperm quality.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Trigonella foenum graecum capsules may help improve sperm count by enhancing sperm motility and reducing oxidative stress.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, in Trigonella foenum graecum capsules have antioxidant properties that may help protect sperm cells from damage and improve sperm count.

Fenugreek saponins (FSG): Fenugreek saponins (FSG) in Trigonella foenum graecum capsules have been shown to improve sperm count, motility, and morphology by enhancing testosterone levels and reducing oxidative stress.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum seeds and sift through a fine mesh.
Grind the sifted seeds into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 25 grams of the ground powder with 125 grams of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and dispense into capsules using a 500-count machine.
Label 100 capsules with the name trigonella foenum graecum and the dosage of 500 milligrams per capsule.

The capsules also contain other nutrients like calcium, magnesium, and potassium that are essential for healthy sperm production. This natural supplement may help increase sperm count and improve fertility in men with low sperm counts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cinnamaldehyde present in Ceylon cinnamon has anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce inflammation in the testes and epididymis, promoting healthy sperm production and motility.

Antidiabetic properties: Ceylon cinnamon's ability to regulate blood sugar levels may help improve insulin sensitivity and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to low sperm count and poor fertility.
remedy_constituents: Lignans: Lignans, a type of phenolic compound found in Cinnamomum verum, have antioxidant properties that help protect sperm cells from oxidative stress and damage, thus contributing to improved sperm count.

Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde, a key component of Ceylon Cinnamon, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the reproductive system, which can lead to improved sperm count and motility.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin, a type of flavonoid present in Cinnamomum verum, acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation that can negatively impact sperm count and quality.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which is believed to improve sperm quality and count.

remedy_recipe: Dry the cinnamomum verum bark in a low-temperature oven at 150f for 2 hours to preserve its potency.
Grind 100g of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 5g of the ground cinnamomum verum powder with 95g of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Use a capsule filler to fill 100 capsules with 500mg of the cinnamomum verum and cellulose mixture.
Store the cinnamomum verum capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a single layer on a paper towel for 7 to 10 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Mix the powder with a capsule filler such as rice flour or potato starch in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill the powdered mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler machine.
Label the capsules with the name of the supplement and the dosage which is typically 240 milligrams per serving.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barrenwort capsules helps with low sperm count because they contain a unique blend of herbs that stimulate the production of healthy sperm. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality epimedium sagittatum roots in the morning after sunrise when their potency is at its peak.
Dry the harvested roots for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content and preserve their active compounds.
Grind dried epimedium sagittatum roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix 200mg of ground epimedium sagittatum powder with 100mg of gelatin or other natural binding agent and fill into empty capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years to preserve potency.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ophiopogon japonicus roots in late summer or early fall at dawn when dew is still present on the leaves.

Store the filled herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze them for longer storage.

Grind the dried schisandra chinensis into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of the ground schisandra chinensis with 200mg of ginseng root powder and 100mg of astragalus membranaceus root powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filler machine or spoon and press the excess air out for 10 seconds.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to 2 years.

Additionally, turmeric's ability to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body may help protect the reproductive system from damage, leading to an increase in sperm count and overall fertility.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can improve sperm quality and motility by protecting sperms from damage caused by free radicals.

Antioxidative Effect on Sperm DNA: Curcumin has been shown to scavenge free radicals that can damage sperm DNA, thereby reducing DNA fragmentation and improving sperm quality, which is crucial for fertility.

Anti-Androgenic Effects: Turmeric's curcumin has been found to reduce the levels of testosterone, a hormone that can contribute to low sperm count by inhibiting spermatogenesis (the production of sperm). By regulating testosterone levels, turmeric may help improve sperm production and quality.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are the underground stems that store nutrients and have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat male infertility due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain a high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound shown to improve sperm quality and motility by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots curcuma longa of high quality and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the turmeric roots in a well-ventilated area for at least seven days or until they become brittle.
Grind 200 grams of dried turmeric powder into a fine consistency using a spice grinder for five minutes.
Mix one part of the ground turmeric with two parts of vegetable filler such as potato starch and capsule powder to create a uniform blend.
Fill hard gelatin capsules with the blended mixture, each containing 400 milligrams of curcuma longa extract, and store them in an airtight container.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to low sperm count that are treated with herbal capsules are male infertility, erectile dysfunction, premature ejaculation, and impotence. Herbs like Ashwagandha, Tribulus Terrestris, and Withania Somnifera have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to improve sperm quality, increase libido, and enhance fertility. These herbs may also help alleviate symptoms of ED and PE, promoting overall sexual health and well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with low sperm count is a blend of Tribulus Terrestris, Ashwagandha, and Maca. Tribulus Terrestris enhances testosterone production and improves sperm count, while Ashwagandha reduces stress and anxiety that can negatively impact fertility. Maca, a plant-based adaptogen, boosts sperm count and motility. Combining these herbs may help improve semen quality, increase fertility, and promote overall reproductive health. This natural blend can be taken under the guidance of a healthcare professional to support fertility and overall well-being.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current drug discovery technologies, the use of herbal medicines has become popular in treating male infertility due to their high effectiveness and low cost. The study conducted a systematic review of 14 trials with 1218 participants, and found that 12 out of 15 plants and medicinal products were effective in treating idiopathic male infertility. Specifically, herbal capsules such as Y virilin capsule and manix capsule, and other herbal medicines like Withania somnifera roots, Alpinia officinarum, Nigella sativa seeds, and Ceratonia siliqua, have been found to improve male fertility, which can be beneficial for individuals with low sperm count.
